param.angles: The default angles of the camera.
param.fov: The default field of view of the camera.

canexitvehicle.description:
Called when a player is attempting to leave an engine vehicle (e.g. a chair). 
This hook is executed only if the player has granted the CONTROL_VEHICLE_INPUT permission and the vehicle belongs to the instance owner.

param.ply: The player that is attempting to leave the vehicle.
param.vehicle: The vehicle the player is currently sat in.

chatboxclosed.description:
Called on a HUD when you are linked to it and the chatbox has been closed. 
This requires the READ_CHATBOX_STATE permission.

chatboxopened.description:
Called on a HUD when you are linked to it and the chatbox has been opened. 
This requires the READ_CHATBOX_STATE permission.

chattextchanged.description:
Called on a HUD when you are linked to it and the text in the chatbox input has changed. 
This requires the READ_CHATBOX_STATE permission.

param.text: The contents of the chatbox input.

checkfiledownload.ret: True to allow the download, false to deny.
checkfiledownload.description:
Called when a file is requested by a client from an asynchronous include. 
Return false to deny the download. If no hook callback returns a value, the download will be allowed.

param.fileName: The name of the file being downloaded (as a full path).

checktricorderoverride.ret: True if they should be allowed to use it, false otherwise.

dtvarchanged.description: Called when a DTVar value is changed (if change detection was enabled by net.listenForDTVarChanges() or net.listenForAllDTVarChanges()).

param.index: The index under the given type of the DTVar that has changed.

param.newValue: The new value for the DTVar.

entityremoved.description: Called when an entity is removed.

entitytakedamage.description:
Called when an entity takes damage through the server's damage system. 
NOTE: This hook is sampled over time, and will not report every damage event. To track exact damage amounts, periodically poll the health of a given entity.

param.inflictor: The weapon that caused the damage.
param.attacker: The owner of the weapon that caused the damage.
param.pos: The position at which the damage occurred (in world space).

hudlink.description: Called when a player links to your HUD (clientside only).

hudshoulddraw.description:
Called on a HUD when you are linked to it. 
Return a value to deny the given HUD element from drawing. 
See: https://wiki.facepunch.com/gmod/HUD_Element_List 
In addition to the above, the following extra elements are defined: 
* LuaHudHealth 
* LuaHudAmmo 
* LuaHudDeathNotice

param.element: The string name of the HUD element requesting permission to draw.

hudunlink.description: Called when a player unlinks from your HUD (clientside only).

input.description: Called when an input on a wired SF chip is written to

param.value: The value of the input

inputmouseapply.description: Called when mouse input is applied to the local player's view.

param.x: The amount of mouse movement along the x-axis this frame.
param.y: The amount of mouse movement along the y-axis this frame.
param.viewAngles: The view angles of the local player.

keypress.description: Called when a player presses an input button.

param.ply: The player pressing the button.

keyrelease.description: Called when a player releases an input button.

param.ply: The player releasing the button.

net.description:
Called when a network message is received. 
Note that network messages are stateful, and thus once read, data cannot be retrieved again. Having multiple callbacks for this hook will not work unless each is reading a separate part of the incoming message.

param.length: The length of the network message in bits.
param.player: The player that sent the network message (only on the server side).

networkentitycreated.description: Called when a networked entity has been created.

param.entity: The entity that has been created.

notifyshouldtransmit.description: Called when an entity enters or leaves the local player's PVS.

param.entity: The entity whose transmission state has changed.
param.shouldTransmit: Whether the entity is transmitting or not.

oncontextmenuclose.description: Called when the GMod context menu is closed (-menu_context keybind).

oncontextmenuopen.description: Called when the GMod context menu is opened (+menu_context keybind).

onentitycreated.description: Called when an entity is created.

onentityscanstarted.description:
Called when the local player starts scanning an entity with a scanning weapon. 
Note that this may be called multiple times with different entities as the player switches targets before "onentityscanstopped" is called. 
To detect scanning on players other than the instance owner, a linked HUD is required.

param.ply: The player that is initiating the scan.
param.scanner: The scanner weapon being used to scan the entity.
param.target: The target entity that is being scanned.

onentityscanstopped.description:
Called when the local player stops scanning an entity with a scanning weapon. 
Note that this is called only when the player no longer has an active scanning target. If the player changes target, only "onentityscanstarted" will be called. 
To detect scanning on players other than the instance owner, a linked HUD is required.

param.ply: The player that was scanning.
param.scanner: The scanner weapon that was being used to scan an entity.

onremove.description: Called when the chip is being removed or updated with new code.

param.reason: The reason for the instance shutdown (one of "UPDATING", "RESTARTING", "TERMINATING" or "REMOVING").

onscreensizechanged.description:
Called when the game's screen resolution has changed. 
Calling render.getScreenRes() will return the new size.

param.oldWidth: The old screen width in pixels.
param.oldHeight: The old screen height in pixels.

permissiongranted.description: Runs when a permission has been granted to this instance.

param.permission: The permission that has been granted.
param.player: The player that granted the permission. This is always ents.player() on the client.

permissionrevoked.description: Runs when a permission has been revoked from this instance.

param.permission: The permission that has been revoked.
param.player: The player that revoked the permission. This is always ents.player() on the client.

playerattempteduse.description:
Called when a player attempts to use an entity. 
This is called before prop protection checks, so the player may not be allowed to use the entity. Use the "playeruse" hook for successful use events.

param.ply: The player that pressed their use key.
param.ent: The entity that the player is attempting to use.

playerauthed.description: Called when a player's Steam ID has been confirmed.

param.ply: The player that has been authed.
param.uniqueID: Don't use it, it's not really unique thanks to Garry.

playerbindpress.description: Called when a player runs a command. Return true to block the command (only works for the chip's owner).

param.ply: The player using the command (the local player).
param.pressed: Boolean value whether the bind was activated or deactivated.

playerbuttondown.description: Called when a player presses down any button.

param.ply: The player pressing the button.

playerbuttonup.description: Called when a player releases any button.

param.ply: The player releasing the button.

param.inflictor: The entity that caused the death.

param.ply: The player that left.

playerenteredvehicle.description: Called when a player enters an engine vehicle (e.g. a chair).

param.ply: The player that entered the vehicle.
param.seatIndex: The index of the seat that was entered (starting from 0).

playerinitialspawn.description: Called when a player spawns for the first time.

param.ply: The player that has spawned for the first time.

playerleavevehicle.description: Called when a player leaves an engine vehicle (e.g. a chair), after any exit animation is completed.

param.ply: The player that left the vehicle.

playeruse.description: Called when a player successfully uses an entity after passing prop protection checks.

param.ply: The player that pressed their use key.
param.ent: The entity that is being used.

postduplicationpaste.description: Called when the duplication the chip is a part of has been fully restored.

param.createdEntities: A table keyed by entity index (at the point of saving), where each entity corresponds to an entity in the dupe.

postentitypaste.description:
Called when the chip has been restored. 
Note that other entities in the duplication may not have been fully restored yet. To wait until everything has been restored, use the "postduplicationpaste" hook.

param.data: The table of data you saved with PreEntityCopy.
param.createdEntities: A table keyed by entity index (at the point of saving), where each entity corresponds to an entity in the dupe.

preentitycopy.description:
Called when the chip is being copied. 
Note: don't try to store anything other than basic numbers, booleans and strings. To store an entity, store its entity index, then use the created entities lookup in PostEntityPaste using the saved index.

param.data: Add values to this table to be saved.

render.description:
Called when a frame is requested to be drawn. You may want to unhook from this if you don't need 
to render anything for a bit

renderoverlay.description:
Called on HUDs and emitters when they need to render their overlay (i.e. when the local player is looking at the chip). 
Adding a hook for this event will hide the default overlay. If you just want to disable the overlay from drawing, use starfall.showOverlay( false ).

param.activator: The player that pressed use on the screen.

tick.description: Called once per game tick on both the client and the server.

Gets the average CPU time for the current instance, used for the average CPU time quota. 
This value represents a moving average over previous frames/ticks and is not updated in real time.

xpcall.usage:
local function ErrorHandler( Error ) 
	-- Here the stack is yet to unwind, so the traceback points at the exact line the error occurred. 
	-- The value returned here will be output as the second returned value from xpcall. 
	return debug.traceback( Error ) 
end 
local function MyAwesomeFunc( ... ) 
	print( "Thanks for", ... ) 
end 
local Success, Err = xpcall( MyAwesomeFunc, ErrorHandler, "cake", "cookies", "milk" ) 
if not Success then 
	-- Err will include an accurate traceback here due to returning debug.traceback() in the error handler. 
	print( "Failed with error:", Err ) 
end
xpcall.description: Runs the given function and calls the given callback function on error.

param.callback: Function to run on error. It's passed the error and executed before the stack unwinds, allowing for accurate tracebacks. The returned value is used as the final error result.

create.ret: Coroutine object representing the given function.
create.usage:
-- Create a new thread that does some work. 
local Thread = coroutine.create( function( Message ) 
	print( "Caller says:", Message ) 
	local Reply = coroutine.yield( "Hello!" ) 
	print( "Caller says:", Reply ) 
	return "Goodbye!" 
end ) 
local Success, Message = coroutine.resume( Thread, "Hello thread!" ) 
assert( Success ) 
print( "Coroutine says:", Message ) 
Success, Message = coroutine.resume( Thread, "Goodbye thread!" ) 
assert( Success ) 
print( "Coroutine says:", Message ) 
-- Coroutine is now dead as its function has returned.
create.description: Creates and returns a coroutine thread object from the given function.

param.func: Function to run inside the coroutine.

ret: True if the coroutine executed without error, false if an error was caught.
If the coroutine errored, the error that was thrown. Otherwise, all values passed to coroutine.yield() or returned by the coroutine's function.
resume.usage:
-- Pass some data to a thread and retrieve its next yielded response. 
local Success, Data = coroutine.resume( Thread, { Message = "Hello thread!" } ) 
-- This data will be taken from the next coroutine.yield() or return statement in the thread. 
print( Data )

The parameters to pass to the coroutine (as the initial arguments, or to be returned by coroutine.yield()).

running.usage:
-- Within a coroutine body, it's possible to get the running thread. 
coroutine.wrap( function() 
	local Thread = coroutine.running() 
	print( coroutine.status( Thread ) ) -- "running" 
end )() 
-- Outside of a coroutine, there is no running thread. 
print( coroutine.running() ) -- nil
running.description: Returns the current running coroutine, or nil if none is running.

status.ret: The status of the given coroutine.
status.usage:
-- New coroutines are suspended. 
local Thread = coroutine.create( function() end ) 
print( coroutine.status( Thread ) ) -- "suspended"
status.description: Gets the status of a given coroutine. Either 'suspended', 'running', 'normal' or 'dead'.

wait.usage:
-- Every second, print the next number in the sequence. 
local Thread = coroutine.create( function() 
	local i = 1 
	while true do 
		coroutine.wait( 1 ) 
		print( i ) 
		i = i + 1 
	end 
end ) 
-- Poll the coroutine to allow it to advance time. 
hook.add( "think", "coroutine_counter", function() coroutine.resume( Thread ) end )
wait.description:
Yields the current coroutine and will continue to yield when called until the given time has elapsed (relative to time.curTime()). 
This does not resume the coroutine for you after the given time. You must keep resuming it until the time has elapsed (e.g. in a think hook or repeating timer).

param.durationInSeconds: The time in seconds to yield for.

wrap.ret: Function that resumes the coroutine when invoked, returning any yielded/returned values.
wrap.usage:
-- Generate an iterator over powers of 2 using a coroutine-wrapped function. 
-- Each invocation in a generic for will resume the coroutine. 
local function PowersOf2( Start, End ) 
	return coroutine.wrap( function() 
		for i = Start, End do 
			coroutine.yield( bit.lshift( 1, i ) ) 
		end 
	end ) 
end 
-- This will print every power of 2 between 2 ^ 1 and 2 ^ 10 (inclusive). 
for PowerOf2 in PowersOf2( 1, 10 ) do 
	print( PowerOf2 ) 
end
wrap.description:
Returns a function that, when invoked, resumes the coroutine representing the given function, passing the provided arguments. 
The returned values from the wrapped function represent the values that were yielded from the coroutine, or returned at the end.

param.func: Function to run inside the coroutine.

yield.ret: The values passed to coroutine.resume() or a coroutine-wrapped function after this yield.
yield.usage:
-- Yield some data to the caller, and use the data passed through coroutine.resume() afterwards. 
local Data = coroutine.yield( { Message = "Hello caller!" } ) 
-- This next line will not execute until the caller resumes the thread again. 
print( Data )
yield.description:
Yields the current coroutine, suspending it in place ready for the next resume. 
Suspended coroutines will not execute again until they are resumed externally.

The values to yield to the caller, which will be returned by coroutine.resume() (or from calling a coroutine-wrapped function).

traceback.ret: A string representing the current call stack, or the given message if it is not a string.

-- Get a traceback starting at the current line

param.thread: (Optional) The coroutine to get the traceback from.

param.stackLevel: (Optional) The stack level to start the traceback from (1 = where debug.traceback() is called or where a coroutine last yielded/died).

addHalo.ret:
A table with the following editable fields (case-insensitive): 
- Color 
- BlurX 
- BlurY 
- DrawPasses 
- Additive 
- IgnoreZ 
Editing any of these values will update the halo for all the holograms passed in originally.
addHalo.usage:
-- Adds a very blurry yellow halo around the first hologram in holograms.getAll() 
-- Note that halos are persistent until you call Hologram:removeHalo(). 
-- Note also that additive should really be always true, otherwise you'll get an odd looking halo. 
local Holos = holograms.getAll() 
holograms.addHalo( Holos[ 1 ], Color( 255, 255, 0 ), 10, 10, 1, true, false ) 
 
-- Alternatively, you can also add halos to multiple holograms at once. 
-- This will add a sharp red halo to all holograms the chip has spawned. 
local HaloParameters = holograms.addHalo( Holos, Color( 255, 0, 0 ), 1, 1, 1, true, false ) 
-- After 1 second, make the halos draw through walls, and increase the blur. 
timer.simple( 1, function() 
	HaloParameters.IgnoreZ = true 
	HaloParameters.BlurX = 5 
	HaloParameters.BlurY = 5 
end )
addHalo.description:
Adds a halo to all the passed in holograms. A hologram can only have a single halo at a time. 
Halo rendering requires the permission DRAW_HALOS.

param.holos: A single hologram or an array of holograms to have a halo added to.
param.blurX: (Optional, default 2) The amount to blur horizontally, clamped between 0 and 10.
param.blurY: (Optional, default 2) The amount to blur vertically, clamped between 0 and 10.
param.drawPasses: (Optional, default 1) The amount of passes to make when drawing the halo. A higher value reduces blur. Clamped between 1 and 3.
param.additive: (Optional, default true) Whether the halo should be additively rendered.
param.ignoreZ: (Optional, default false) Whether the halo should be occluded by objects in the way or not.

generate.usage:
-- Generates a 64x64 white rectangle at (0, 0) 
local function GenerateMesh() 
	mesh.writeQuadFromPlane( Vector( 32, 32, 0 ), Vector( 0, 0, -1 ), 64, 64 ) 
end 
-- In a render hook, call mesh.generate to draw the mesh dynamically. 
-- Alternatively, pass an IMesh to store the mesh data to be drawn later. 
hook.add( "render", "mesh", function() 
	render.clear( 0, 0, 0, 255 ) 
	mesh.generate( mesh.MATERIAL_QUADS, 1, GenerateMesh ) 
end )
generate.description:
Generates a mesh, either writing to the given mesh object, or otherwise drawing the result directly. 
Writing to an IMesh can be done outside a rendering hook. 
Note that you cannot yield in the callback as mesh generation must occur synchronously in a single frame.

param.primitiveType: The type of primitives to draw (mesh.MATERIAL_*).
param.primitiveCount: The number of primitives that will be drawn.
param.generatorFunc: A function that, when called, should write the mesh.
param.meshInstance: An optional IMesh object to write the mesh values to.

param.position: The centre point for the quad.
param.color: (Optional) The colour to apply to each vertex in the quad (default is white).

writeQuadFromVertices.description: Writes 4 vertices to the mesh, all with the given colour.

param.color: (Optional) The colour to apply to each vertex in the quad (default is white).

writeTexCoord.description: Sets the texture co-ordinates of the current vertex.

param.stage: The stage to write the co-ordinates to. Stage 0 is the main texture, stages 1 and 2 are used for lightmaps.

writeUserData.description: Sets the user data for the current vertex. This is used to define the tangent.

param.tangentX: The x component of the tangent vector.
param.tangentY: The y component of the tangent vector.
param.tangentZ: The z component of the tangent vector.
param.tangentHandedness: The handedness of the tangent vector (either -1 or 1).

listenForAllDTVarChanges.description:
Enables change detection for all DataTable values on the chip. 
Changes will be broadcast via. the "dtvarchanged" hook, passing the type, index, old value and new value. 
This can increase CPU usage, so should only be used if all DataTable values are used and need change detection. 
Use net.listenForDTVarChanges() to specify more precise change detection.

Enables change detection for the given DataTable value.

setDTVar.description:
Sets a DataTable value on the given chip entity. 
DataTable values are networked automatically to all clients whenever their value changes. 
Each chip has a limited number of values available per type. The index controls which value to set under a given type. 
If you omit the last argument, the value will be set on the chip executing this function. 
For entities, use ents.NULL_ENTITY to reset the value.

param.type: Type of the DTVar. One of "Angle", "Bool", "Entity", "Float", "Int", "String", or "Vector".
param.index: An integer between 1 and 32 (1 and 4 for strings).
param.value: The value to set.
param.chip: (Optional) Chip entity that you own to set the value on.

writeTable.description:
Writes an entire table with any arbitrary structure to the net message. 
NOTE: This writes 8 bit key type, key, 8 bit value type, value for every key-value pair. Usually it's much more efficient to write data in a pre-defined structure, use this as a last resort only.

writeType.description:
Writes the value's type, then its value to the net message. 
Note that this means an extra byte for the type is sent, so use only if necessary. Most net messages should have a pre-defined structure and not need to use this function. 
This is especially expensive if writing an arbitrary table, as it will use net.writeTable() internally.

param.value: The value to write.

param.bits: Number of bits the integer uses. Must be between 1 and 32.

writeVector.description: Writes a vector to the net message.

param.lowPrecision: If true, the written data will be smaller at the cost of precision.

All valid permissions are fields on the permissions library table.

permissions.description:
Permissions library. 
Provides an interface to check for access to certain priveleged functions.

SET_PLAYER_POSITION.description: Permission to set the owner's position.

USE_RENDER_TARGET_MATERIALS_WITH_HOLOGRA.description: Permission to apply render target textures as a hologram's material.

swap.description:
Performs a pop and a push simultaneously, without discarding the starting time of the last state. 
Thus, the new state starts from the old state's ending time and the old state stops here.

param.name: The name of the new state.

profiler.description: Profiler library for recording execution time.

create.description:

Can optionally place it at a given position, and set its angles.

param.model: The model to give the vehicle (must be a valid seat model, see the vehicles tab of the spawnmenu).

New.ret: A new quaternion whose values depend on the provided input.
New.description: Creates a new Quaternion given a variety of inputs

A series of arguments which lead to valid generation of a quaternion. 
Acceptable inputs are: 
- A single number for the real part. 
- 4 numbers for the real and 3 imaginary parts. 
- A vector which populates the imaginary parts. 
- An angle object. 
- A number and a vector to populate the real and imaginary parts. 
- 2 vectors to populate with the angle of the cross product between them. 
- An entity which populates using the entity's angles.

abs.ret: The absolute value of the given quaternion.
abs.description: Returns the absolute value of q.

param.q: The quaternion to get the absolute value of.

conj.ret: The conjugate of the given quaternion.

param.q: The quaternion to get the conjugate of.

exp.ret: e raised to the power of the given quaternion.
exp.description: Returns Euler's constant e raised to the power q.

param.q: The quaternion to raise e to the power of.

fromAngles.ret: A quaternion representing the given angles.
fromAngles.description: Creates a quaternion representing the given angles.

fromAxis.ret: A quaternion representing the world angles of the basis' forward direction, accounting for roll.
fromAxis.description: Creates a quaternion representing the angles expressed by the given basis vectors.

param.forward: The forward direction of the basis.
param.up: The up direction of the basis.

fromComponents.ret: A quaternion with the given components.
fromComponents.description: Creates a quaternion from the given components.

fromEntity.ret: A quaternion representing the angles of the given entity.
fromEntity.description: Creates a quaternion representing the angles of the given entity.

param.entity: The entity whose angles should be used.

fromNumber.ret: A quaternion whose real part is equal to the given value, with no imaginary parts.
fromNumber.description: Creates a quaternion with real part equal to the given number.

fromNumberAndVector.ret: A quaternion whose real part is the given number, and imginary parts are taken from the given vector (i, j, k).
fromNumberAndVector.description: Creates a quaternion whose real part is the given number, and whose imaginary parts are taken from the given vector.

param.imaginaryParts: The imaginary parts as a Vector.

fromVector.ret: A quaternion whose real part is 0, and imginary parts are taken from the given vector (i, j, k).
fromVector.description: Creates a quaternion with imaginary parts set from the given vector.

param.imaginaryParts: The imaginary parts as a Vector.

inv.ret: The inverse of the given quaternion.

param.q: The quaternion to get the inverse of.

log.ret: The natural logarithm of the given quaternion.
log.description: Returns the natural logarithm of q.

param.q: The quaternion to apply the natural logarithm to.

qMod.ret: A new quaternion with a normalised angle.
qMod.description: Changes quaternion q so that the represented rotation is by an angle between 0 and 180 degrees.

qRotation.ret: A quaternion that, when applied to a vector, rotates it by the given direction and magnitude in degrees.
qRotation.description:
Constructs a quaternion from the rotation vector rotVec. 
Vector direction is axis of rotation, magnitude is angle in degrees.

param.rotVec: The rotation vector to be represented as a quaternion.

qRotationAxis.ret: A quaternion that, when applied to a vector, rotates it around the given axis by the given angle in degrees.
qRotationAxis.description: Returns a quaternion for rotation about the given axis by the given angle.

qi.ret: A quaternion whose i component is n and all others are 0.

param.n: The value of the i component.

qj.ret: A quaternion whose j component is n and all others are 0.

param.n: The value of the j component.

qk.ret: A quaternion whose k component is n and all others are 0.

param.n: The value of the k component.

rotationAngle.ret: The angle of rotation represented by the given quaternion, in degrees.
rotationAngle.description: Returns the angle of rotation in degrees.

param.q: The quaternion to extract the rotation angle from.
The quaternion to extract the rotation axis from.

rotationVector.ret: A vector representing the rotation vector of the given quaternion.
rotationVector.description: Returns the rotation vector represented by the given quaternion, where the magnitude is the angle of rotation in degress.

param.q: The quaternion to extract the rotation vector from.

slerp.ret: A quaternion that is between q0 and q1, spherically interpolated based on the value of t.
slerp.description: Performs spherical interpolation between q0 and q1. Returns q0 for t = 0, q1 for t = 1.

param.t: The fraction between the 2 quaternions (between 0 and 1).

vec.ret: A vector whose components are the i, j, k components of the quaternion.
vec.description: Converts q to a vector by dropping the real component.

param.q: The quaternion to extract the imaginary parts from.

addBeam.description:
Adds a beam control point to the ongoing multi-segment beam rendering operation, if called within a beam generator function. 
Attempting to call this outside of a beam generator function, or to add more control points than were declared will result in an error being thrown.

param.pos: The position of the control point.
param.width: The width to interpolate to/from on this control point.
param.textureEnd: The texture co-ordinate to apply at this control point.
param.color: The colour to apply at this control point.

Only works on screens and inside render target drawing operations.

param.g: Green value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).
param.b: Blue value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).
param.a: Alpha value to clear to (0-255, default = 255).
param.clearDepth: Whether to clear the depth buffer (default = false).
param.clearStencil: Whether to clear the stencil buffer (default = false).

param.g: Green value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).
param.b: Blue value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).
param.a: Alpha value to clear to (0-255, default = 255).

param.y: The y co-ordinate in screen space of the top-left corner of the rectangle.

computeTextureCoords.usage:
local texture = render.getTextureID( "some/material" ) 
-- Compute the corrected texture co-ordinates to tile the texture in a 2x2 grid. 
local u0, v0, u1, v1 = render.computeTextureCoords( texture, 0, 0, 2, 2 ) 
hook( "render", "example", function() 
	render.clear() 
	render.setColor( 255, 255, 255 ) 
	render.setTexture( texture ) 
	render.drawTexturedRectUV( 0, 0, 512, 512, u0, v0, u1, v1 ) 
end )
computeTextureCoords.description:
Computes corrected texture co-ordinates for the given texture/material. 
When using drawTexturedRectUV, the engine internally alters the texture co-ordinates. 
This function accounts for that alteration and ensures the rendering operation uses the intended values.

param.texture: The texture ID or IMaterial to compute corrected co-ordinates for.

createFont.ret: The name of the font that was created. Use this in later rendering functions.
createFont.description:
Creates a font. Cannot be used in a rendering hook, use it outside. Returns the font's unique name. 
This only needs to be called once. After that, use the returned name to reference the font. 
If using a table of parameters, only the first argument is used. The rest are ignored. 
Creating a font is a costly process, only create the fonts you absolutely need.

param.font: Base font name to use OR a table containing font parameters (see the Font structure).
param.size: The size of the font in pixels (between 4 and 255, default is 13).
param.weight: The weight of the font (how thick it is, minimum of 100, default is 500).
param.antialias: Whether to anti-alias the text, which smooths/blurs the edges (default is true).
param.additive: Whether the font should render additively or not (default is false).
param.shadow: Whether to add a shadow to the font (default is false).
param.outline: Whether to add an outline to the font (default is false).
param.blur: The strength of blurring to apply (between 0 and 10, default is 0).

drawMultiSegmentBeam.usage:
local Ent = ents.self() 
-- Define a generator function outside the render hook, taking parameters. 
local function GenerateExampleBeamSegments( Colour ) 
	-- Inside the generator function, add every declared beam control point. 
	-- This example draws 3 segments (1 -> 2, 2 -> 3 and 3 -> 4). 
	render.addBeam( Ent:toWorld( Vector( 0, 0, 0 ) ), 16, 1, Colour ) 
	render.addBeam( Ent:toWorld( Vector( 0, -32, 0 ) ), 16, 0, Colour ) 
	render.addBeam( Ent:toWorld( Vector( 0, 0, -32 ) ), 16, 1, Colour ) 
	render.addBeam( Ent:toWorld( Vector( 0, -32, -32 ) ), 16, 0, Colour ) 
end 
-- Pick an appropriate material to render the beam with. 
local BeamMaterial = Material( "trails/physbeam" ) 
local White = Color( 255, 255, 255 ) 
hook( "render", "example", function() 
	-- Pass the generator function to drawMultiSegmentBeam to render the beam segments. 
	-- Additional arguments can be passed to the generator to make re-using functions easier. 
	render.setMaterial( BeamMaterial ) 
	render.drawMultiSegmentBeam( 4, GenerateExampleBeamSegments, White ) 
end )
drawMultiSegmentBeam.description:
Draws a multi-segment beam in 3D space using the given generator function to add beam segments. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

param.numControlPoints: The number of control points that will be used to draw beam segments (up to 128, n beam segments require n + 1 control points). Exceeding this value will result in an error. At least 2 control points are required to render a beam.
param.generatorFunc: A function that will add beam segments using render.addBeam(). This function cannot yield.
Arguments to pass to the generator function.

drawPoly.description:
Draws a polygon. Each vertex must be a table with x, y, u and v fields. 
Vertices should be in clockwise order, starting at the upper-left most, and form a convex shape. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

drawQuad.description:
Draws a quad in 3D space. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

drawQuadFromPlane.description:
Draws a quad in 3D space based on the given plane position and normal vector. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

param.pos: The centre position of the quad.
param.normal: The normal vector of the quad (pointing upwards).
param.rotation: (Optional) The rotation in degrees to rotate each corner around the normal vector of the quad, relative to the centre position.

drawRawText.description:
Draws the given text using whatever colour was set previously by setColor(), the position set by 
setTextPos() and the font set by setFont(). 
Unlike drawText, this does not handle alignment, new lines or tabs for you. However, it 
will be significantly more performant. 
After drawing, the text position will be updated to be after the width of the drawn text, so you 
do not need to update it manually unless you wish to render elsewhere. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

drawRect.description:
Draws a rectangle using the current colour. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

drawRectOutline.description:
Draws a rectangle outline using the current colour. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

param.thickness: The thickness of the outline (default = 1). Thicknesses > 1 are slightly more costly to draw.

drawRoundedRect.description:
Draws a rounded rectangle using the current colour. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

param.radius: The radius of the rounded corners. Should be power of 2.

drawSphere.description:
Draws a sphere in 3D space. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

param.pos: The position of the sphere.
param.longitudeSteps: The amount of longitude steps (controls how smooth the sphere is).
param.latitudeSteps: The amount of latitude steps (controls how smooth the sphere is).

drawSprite.description:
Draws a sprite in 3D space. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

param.pos: The position of the sprite.

drawStencil.description: Call this after render.startStencil() to switch to drawing what you want to be contained.

drawText.description:
Draws the given text using whatever colour was set previously by render.setColor(). 
This will handle new lines and tabs for you, but will not be as performant as render.drawRawText(). 
Note that use of tabs is deprecated, and may not work as expected. Align text manually if needed. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

param.xAlignment: (Optional) Text alignment on the x-axis, default is render.TEXT_ALIGN_LEFT.
param.yAlignment: (Optional) Text alignment on the y-axis, default is render.TEXT_ALIGN_TOP.

drawTextRotated.description:
Draws the given text using whatever colour was set previously by setColor(). 
The text is rotated around its central point by the given angle. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

drawTexturedRect.description:
Draws a textured rectangle. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

drawTexturedRectRotated.description:
Draws a rotated, textured rectangle. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

param.x: X co-ordinate of centre of the rectangle (around which it will be rotated)
param.y: Y co-ordinate of centre of the rectangle (around which it will be rotated)

drawTexturedRectUV.description:
Draws a textured rectangle with UV co-ordinates 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

drawWireframeBox.description:

param.pos: The position of the box.
param.mins: The mins vector for the box.
param.maxs: The maxs vector for the box.
param.writeZ: (Optional) Whether to render the box using the depth buffer (default = false).

drawWireframeSphere.description:
Draws a wireframe sphere in 3D space. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

param.pos: The position of the sphere.
param.longitudeSteps: The amount of longitude steps (controls how smooth the sphere is).
param.latitudeSteps: The amount of latitude steps (controls how smooth the sphere is).
param.writeZ: (Optional) Whether to render the sphere using the depth buffer (default = false). This value is always true when rendering in the world (e.g. with an emitter or hologram).

enableAlpha.description: Sets whether to write alpha when rendering.

param.shouldWrite: Whether to write to the alpha channel.

enableClipping.description:
Enables/disables clipping. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

param.enable: Sets whether clipping is enabled. You should disable it after.

enableColor.usage:
render.startStencil() 
-- Turn off writing colour values. 
render.enableColor( false ) 
-- Render your bounds here 
-- Turn back on writing colour values. 
render.enableColor( true ) 
render.drawStencil() 
-- Draw things constrained to the stencil 
render.endStencil()
enableColor.description:
Sets whether to write colour values. 
Use this to render to the stencil/depth buffer without it being visible. 
NOTE: The engine does not render in 2D if the current colour's alpha is 0, even if colour writing is disabled. You must have a non-zero alpha value set to render to the stencil/depth buffer.

param.shouldWrite: Whether to write colour values or not.

enableDepth.description:
Sets whether to write to the depth buffer. 
Note that "enabling" depth writing here defers to the current rendering context's depth writing mode, which may not 
actually be enabled. Use render.overrideDepthEnable() to force depth writing.

param.shouldWrite: Whether to write to the depth buffer.

ret: The screen-space x co-ordinate of the rect's top-left corner, after applying the current render matrix.
The screen-space y co-ordinate of the rect's top-left corner, after applying the current render matrix.
The screen-space x co-ordinate of the rect's bottom-right corner, after applying the current render matrix.
The screen-space y co-ordinate of the rect's bottom-right corner, after applying the current render matrix.
enableScissorRect.description:
Enables a scissor rect, which will prevent anything drawing outside the provided bounds. 
If a render matrix has been previously pushed, it will be applied to the given co-ordinate values to produce a screen-space scissor rectangle. 
Note that scissor rectanges are axis-aligned, and thus only translation and scaling make sense. 
For more complex use cases, see render.startStencil()/render.drawStencil()/render.cutOutStencil(). 
Allowed render contexts: 2D (or cameras pushed on top of a 2D context).

param.x: The x co-ordinate of the rect's top-left corner.
param.y: The y co-ordinate of the rect's top-left corner.
param.endX: The x co-ordinate of the rect's bottom-right corner.
param.endY: The y co-ordinate of the rect's bottom-right corner.

getTextureID.description:
Looks up a texture ID by file name. The returned ID can be passed to render.setTexture(). 
When passed a PNG file path, the resulting material is equivalent to Material( texture, "noclamp" ). 
For other materials, the returned ID will correspond to a material that can be used in a 2D rendering context using 
the "UnlitGeneric" shader. 
For materials that already use the "UnlitGeneric" shader, use the Material() constructor instead of this function to preserve the original material parameters.

isHUD.description: Returns whether the SF instance is a HUD.

overrideBlend.description: Enables/disables overriding the current blending function.

param.sourceBlend: The blending factor for the source colour values (one of render.BLEND_*).
param.destBlend: The blending factor for the destination colour values (one of render.BLEND_*).
param.blendFunc: The blending function to use for colour (one of render.BLENDFUNC_*).
param.sourceBlendAlpha: The blending factor for the source alpha value (one of render.BLEND_*).

param.blendFuncAlpha: The blending function to use for alpha (one of render.BLENDFUNC_*).

overrideDepthEnable.usage:
-- Force depth writing to be enabled (must be done outside the 3D2D block). 
render.overrideDepthEnable( true, true ) 
 
-- First render a red rectangle. 
render.start3D2D( Pos, Ang, 0.1 ) 
    render.setColor( 255, 0, 0 ) 
    render.drawRect( 0, 0, 256, 256 ) 
render.end3D2D() 
 
-- Then render a yellow rectange behind it, note how this rectangle is occluded. 
-- If you comment out the overrideDepthEnable above, this will draw over the top of the red rectangle. 
render.start3D2D( Pos - Dir * 5, Ang, 0.1 ) 
    render.setColor( 255, 255, 0 ) 
    render.drawRect( 0, 0, 256, 256 ) 
render.end3D2D() 
 
-- Stop overriding depth writing. 
render.overrideDepthEnable( false )
overrideDepthEnable.description:
Allows overriding the default depth write behaviour. 
This can be used to make opaque rendered elements in 3D2D properly occlude each other.

param.enableOverride: Whether to override depth writing behaviour.
param.shouldWrite: Whether depth writing should be enabled. Has no effect if the override is not enabled.

popCustomClipPlane.description:
Pops a clip plane from the clipping plane stack. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

popFilterMag.description: Pops a magnification texture filter from the texture filter stack.

popFilterMin.description: Pops a minification texture filter from the texture filter stack.

popMatrix.description:
Pops a matrix from the current matrix stack. 
Note that 3D2D cameras have their own separate matrix stack, thus attempting to pop a matrix 
pushed before starting a 3D2D camera will result in an error, and any matrices not popped at the 
end of a 3D2D camera will be popped automatically.

pushCustomClipPlane.description:
Pushes a clip plane onto the clipping plane stack. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

param.normal: The normal vector of the plane.
param.distance: The distance of the plane from the origin along the given normal vector.

pushFilterMag.description:
Pushes a magnification texture filter onto the texture filter stack. This applies when a texture is being rendered 
at a larger size than the texture itself.

param.filterType: (Optional) The filter to apply (one of render.TEXFILTER_*, default = render.TEXFILTER_ANISOTROPIC).

pushFilterMin.description:
Pushes a minification texture filter onto the texture filter stack. This applies when a texture is being rendered at 
a smaller size than the texture itself.

param.filterType: (Optional) The filter to apply (one of render.TEXFILTER_*, default = render.TEXFILTER_ANISOTROPIC).

pushMatrix.description:
Pushes a matrix onto the matrix stack, pre-multiplying by the previously pushed matrix if present. 
Note that 3D2D cameras have their own separate matrix stack, thus attempting to pop a matrix 
pushed before starting a 3D2D camera will result in an error, and any matrices not popped at the 
end of a 3D2D camera will be popped automatically.

readPixel.ret: Colour of the pixel as a colour object.
readPixel.description:
Reads the colour of the specified pixel. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D (render targets only).

resetModelLighting.description:
Resets model lighting.

param.r: The red colour value to use (between 0 and 1, default = 1).
param.g: The green colour value to use (between 0 and 1, default = 1).
param.b: The blue colour value to use (between 0 and 1, default = 1).

set3DMaterialFrame.description: Sets the base texture frame index to use for upcoming render operations using the current 3D material.

param.frame: The 0-based frame index to use (cannot exceed render.getNum3DMaterialFrames()).

setAlphaMultiplier.description: Sets a global alpha multiplier on all upcoming 2D drawing operations.

param.mult: Value between 0 and 1 to multiply alpha by.

Sets the alpha value to draw any models with following this point.

setColorModulation.description:
Sets the colour to draw any models with following this point. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

setLightingMode.description:

setMaterial.description: Sets the material for upcoming 3D space rendering operations.

param.mat: The material to use, or nil to set to the default material.

setMaterialOverride.description: Sets an override material for upcoming 3D space rendering operations. This can be used to override the material on clientside models and holograms.

param.mat: The material to use, or nil to remove any override material.

Sets model lighting from one of 6 directions (see the BOX_ enums).

setStencilCompareFunction.description: Sets the stencil comparison function, which determines which pixels are rendered.

param.stencilComparisonFunction: The comparison function to use (one of render.STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_*).

setStencilEnable.description:
Sets whether stencil testing and writing is enabled or not. 
This is a lower-level interface into the stencil buffer. For most stencil use cases, use render.startStencil() instead. 
Allowed render contexts: all contexts except hologram render callbacks.

param.enable: Whether to enable stencil testing and writing.

setStencilFailOperation.description: Sets the stencil operation to execute when a stencil test fails for a pixel.

param.stencilOperation: The operation to use (one of render.STENCILOPERATION_*).

setStencilPassOperation.description: Sets the stencil operation to execute when a stencil test passes for a pixel.

param.stencilOperation: The operation to use (one of render.STENCILOPERATION_*).

setStencilReferenceValue.description: Sets the stencil reference value to use when comparing pixel values in the stencil buffer.

param.value: The reference value (between 0 and 255).

setStencilTestMask.description: Sets the stencil test mask. All stencil values read from the stencil buffer will be combined with this mask using a bitwise-and operation before they are used in comparison functions.

param.value: The mask to set (a single byte, 0-255). Setting to 255 disables masking.

setStencilWriteMask.description: Sets the stencil write mask. All stencil values written to the stencil buffer will be combined with this mask using a bitwise-and operation before they are committed.

param.value: The mask to set (a single byte, 0-255). Setting to 255 disables masking.

setStencilZFailOperation.description: Sets the stencil operation to execute when a stencil test fails for a pixel due to it being occluded by a previously written pixel in the depth buffer.

param.stencilOperation: The operation to use (one of render.STENCILOPERATION_*).

param.frame: The 0-based frame index to use (cannot exceed render.getNumTextureFrames()).

start3D.description:
Starts a 3D camera context. Used to render 3D models on a 2D surface. 
Calling with no arguments will replicate the current player's view. 
See render.start3DWithParameters for a more advanced version of this function that can handle orthographic projections.

param.pos: The position in the new 3D world to position the camera.
param.fov: The field of view of the camera.
param.x: The X position on the 2D surface to draw the 3D world.
param.y: The Y position on the 2D surface to draw the 3D world.
param.w: The width to take up on the 2D surface.
param.h: The height to take up on the 2D surface.
param.nearZ: The distance from the camera to start the near clipping plane.
param.farZ: The distance from the camera to start the far clipping plane.

start3D2D.description:
Starts a 3D2D camera. Rendering inside this will be drawn in the world at the given location. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

param.pos: Position of the top left corner of the 3D2D camera.
param.scale: Scale multiplier to apply to all rendering within the camera.

start3DWithParameters.description:
Starts a 3D camera using a parameters table describing the camera. 
This can be used for more advanced 3D rendering, such as orthographic projections.

param.cameraParameters: The parameters that describe the camera to start.

startStencil.usage:
-- Start the stencil, and draw a box to contain our content. 
render.startStencil() 
render.setColor( 255, 255, 255 ) 
render.drawRect( 128, 128, 256, 256 ) 
-- Now draw text inside the box. 
render.drawStencil() 
render.setColor( 0, 0, 0 ) 
render.drawText( Font, 256, 256, "Hello World!", render.TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER ) 
-- Finally, end the stencil. 
render.endStencil()
startStencil.description:
Starts a stencil operation, clearing any previous stencil values. 
Anything drawn after this and before calling render.drawStencil() will be used to restrict drawing. 
Anything drawn after this and before calling render.cutOutStencil() will be cut out of what's drawn after. 
For simple 2D box clipping, consider using render.enableScissorRect() instead. 
Allowed render contexts: all contexts except hologram render callbacks.

suppressEngineLighting.description:
Sets whether to supress the standard lighting. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

param.suppressEngineLighting: Whether to suppress the standard lighting.

Returns the location on the screen of a world space position as a table with x, y and boolean visible values. 
This only works on HUDs.

param.pos: World-space position vector you want to know the onscreen location of.

vertex.ret: { x = x or 0, y = y or 0, u = u or 0, v = v or 0 }

vertex.description: Creates a vertex for use with polygons. This just creates a table; it doesn't really do anything special

render.description: Render library. Screens are 512x512 units. Most functions require that you be in the rendering hook to call, otherwise an error is thrown. +x is right, +y is down

STUDIO_RENDER.description: This flag is always set when rendering entities, indicating that the entity should render.

STUDIO_TWOPASS.description:
Indicates that the current render is for an entity that will draw both opaque and translucent renderables (i.e. using RENDERGROUP_BOTH). 
This flag is present on both passes.

STUDIO_TRANSPARENCY.description: Indicates that the current render is for the translucent render pass. Use this flag to differentiate between the opaque and translucent pass when using RENDERGROUP_BOTH.

RENDERGROUP_BOTH.description: Marks the entity as rendering in both the opaque and translucent passes. For holograms, this results in the render callback being invoked twice per frame (once in each pass).

RENDERGROUP_OPAQUE.description: Marks the entity as rendering in just the opaque pass (i.e. the entity is completely opaque).

RENDERGROUP_TRANSLUCENT.description: Marks the entity as rendering in just the translucent pass (i.e. the entity is completely translucent).

MATERIAL_LIGHT_POINT.description: A light that emits from a single point in all directions.

MATERIAL_LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL.description: A light that emits from a point in a specific direction. Unlike a spot light, this does not have multiple cones.

MATERIAL_LIGHT_SPOT.description: A spotlight comprising of 2 light cones.

CAM_3D.description: Constant for a pushed 3D camera context.

TEXFILTER_LINEAR.description: Applies linear texture filtering, applying a basic linear interpolation to each pixel.

TEXFILTER_ANISOTROPIC.description:
Applies anisotropic texture filtering, making textures appear smooth while retaining detail when rendered at an 
angle.

STENCILOPERATION_ZERO.description: Stencil operation to set the stencil value for a pixel to zero.

STENCILOPERATION_REPLACE.description: Stencil operation to replace the stencil value for a pixel with the current reference value.

STENCILOPERATION_INCRSAT.description: Stencil operation to increment the stencil value for a pixel, stopping at 255.

STENCILOPERATION_DECRSAT.description: Stencil operation to decrement the stencil value for a pixel, stopping at 0.

STENCILOPERATION_INVERT.description: Stencil operation to invert the bits in the stencil value for a pixel.

STENCILOPERATION_INCR.description: Stencil operation to increment the stencil value for a pixel, wrapping back to 0 on overflow.

STENCILOPERATION_DECR.description: Stencil operation to decrement the stencil value for a pixel, wrapping back to 255 on overflow.

STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_NEVER.description: Stencil comparison function that never passes.

STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_LESS.description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is less than a pixel's value.

STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_EQUAL.description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is equal to a pixel's value.

STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_LESSEQUAL.description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is less than or equal to a pixel's value.

STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_GREATER.description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is greater than a pixel's value.

STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_NOTEQUAL.description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is not equal to a pixel's value.

STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_GREATEREQUAL.description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is greater than or equal to a pixel's value.

STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_ALWAYS.description: Stencil comparison function that always passes.

BLEND_DST_COLOR.description: Multiplies the given value by the colour value that was previously written.

BLEND_ONE_MINUS_DST_COLOR.description: Multiplies the given value by the colour value that was previously written subtracted from 1.

BLEND_SRC_ALPHA.description: Multiplies the given value by the alpha value being written.

BLEND_ONE_MINUS_SRC_ALPHA.description: Multiplies the given value by the alpha value being written subtracted from 1.

BLEND_DST_ALPHA.description: Multiplies the given value by the alpha value that was previously written.

BLEND_ONE_MINUS_DST_ALPHA.description: Multiplies the given value by the alpha value that was previously written subtracted from 1.

BLEND_SRC_ALPHA_SATURATE.description: Multiplies by the minimum of the source alpha, and (1 - destination alpha).

BLEND_SRC_COLOR.description: Multiplies the given value by the colour value that is being written.

BLEND_ONE_MINUS_SRC_COLOR.description: Multiplies the given value by the colour value that is being written subtracted from 1.

BLENDFUNC_ADD.description: Takes the source and destination values, and adds them together (source + destination).

BLENDFUNC_SUBTRACT.description: Subtracts the destination value from the source value (source - destination).

BLENDFUNC_REVERSE_SUBTRACT.description: Subtracts the source value from the destination value (destination - source).

Flag used to apply self-illumination using the alpha channel when rendering. This applies only to the VertexLitGeneric shader.

Error.ret: An Error object with the given message, capturing the traceback at the given level.
Error.description:
Constructs a new Error object with the given message, capturing the traceback starting at the given level. 
This is akin to a call to error(), but instead of throwing, the message and traceback are captured. 
If the returned error object is thrown with error() and left uncaught, its message and traceback will be shown in the editor, rather than the traceback from the point at which it was thrown.
Error.usage:
local function OnError( Err ) 
	-- Capture the error as-is (level 0 won't add the source to the message), and its traceback. 
	return starfall.Error( Err, 0 ) 
end 
-- Assume that ExampleFunction is some external function whose errors should be captured. 
local Success, Err = xpcall( ExampleFunction, OnError ) 
if not Success then 
	-- Err is an Error type, with a recorded message and traceback. 
	print( "Example operation failed:", Err:getMessage() ) 
	print( Err:getTraceback() ) 
	-- It can be thrown again to preserve the original traceback. 
	error( Err ) 
end

param.message: The message to store on the error (usually a string, but can be any value).
param.level: The stack level at which to start capturing the traceback (default = 1).

-- This snippet loads all script files. 
local Scripts = starfall.getScripts() 
for FilePath, CompiledScript in pairs( Scripts ) do 
	-- If you want to only load those from a certain folder, check FilePath starts with the folder path. 
	-- Calling the CompiledScript function loads the script file. 
	CompiledScript() 
end

The type's own methods are accessible through this table, but cannot be overwritten. 
Note that native types (Angle, Color, Vector) cannot be extended.
getTypeDef.usage:
-- Get the "Player" type. 
local Player = starfall.getTypeDef( "Player" ) 
 
-- Add a new method to check if the player's an ally. 
function Player:isFactionAlly() 
	return faction.isAlly( self ) 
end 
 
-- The new method's ready and can be used on any player object now! 
print( ents.owner():isFactionAlly() ) -- True if you're in a faction.

param.name: The name of the type you want to extend, e.g "Entity".

gsub.description: Substitutes all matches of the given pattern within the given string with the given replacement value, or calls a function for each match if the replacement is a function. See the Lua manual for details on the pattern syntax.
gsub.usage:
-- Replace all matches with a fixed string (this prints "word word", 2). 
print( string.gsub( "test value", "%w+", "word" ) ) 
-- Replace all matches based on a table of values (this prints "first second", 2). 
print( string.gsub( "test value", "%w+", { test = "first", value = "second" } ) ) 
-- Replace all matches by upper casing using a function (this prints "TEST VALUE", 2). 
print( string.gsub( "test value", "%w+", string.upper ) )

param.string: The string to replace values within.
param.pattern: The pattern to find and replace.
param.replacement: If a string, all matches will be replaced with this value. If a table, any matches that have a key in the given table will be replaced with the value under that key. If a function, will be called for each match, passing the capture groups from the pattern as var-args, with a non-nil return value used as the replacement (returning nil will leave the value as-is).
param.max: An optional limit on the number of replacements to perform before stopping (default = unlimited).

* AvatarImage 
* DAdjustableModelPanel 
* DAlphaBar 
* DButton 
* DCollapsibleCategory 
* DCategoryList 
* DCheckBox

* DColorCube 
* DColorMixer 
* DColumnSheet 
* DComboBox 
* DDrawer 
* DFileBrowser 
* DForm 
* DFrame 
* DGrid 
* DHorizontalDivider 
* DHorizontalScroller 
* DIconBrowser 
* DIconLayout 
* DImage 
* DImageButton 
* DLabel 
* DLabelEditable 
* DListView 
* DMenu 
* DMenuBar 
* DModelPanel 
* DNumberScratch 
* DNumberWang 
* DNumPad 
* DNumSlider 
* DPanel 
* DProgress 
* DProperties 
* DPropertySheet 
* DRGBPicker 
* DScrollPanel 
* DTextEntry 
* DTree
