param.class: The class name of the control you want to create.

isCursorVisible.description: Returns whether the mouse cursor is currently visible or not.

isSupportedControl.ret: True if the given class name is supported, false otherwise.
isSupportedControl.description:
Indicates whether the given class is supported and valid to pass to vgui.create(). 
Note that this does not check any additional required permissions, it only indicates whether the class is globally whitelisted for use.

param.class: The class name of the control to check.

vgui.description: VGUI library. Used to create basic VGUI controls. Only available to HUDs.

deserialize.ret: The vON data as a table, or nil if it was not valid vON.
deserialize.description: Deserialises a vON encoded string. Returns the deserialised table.

param.string: A string of vON encoded data.

serialize.ret: A vON string representing the table.
serialize.description: Serialises a table using vON. Returns the serialised string.

create.ret: True if successful, false, error if not.
create.description: Wires the input of Entity1 to the output of Entity2.

param.input: The name of the input on self to wire.
param.output: The name of the output on Ent to wire to.

createInputs.usage:
-- Creates 2 wire inputs, a string and a number. 
wire.createInputs( { "MyStringInput", "MyNumberInput" }, { "string", "normal" }, { "A string input.", "A number input." } )
createInputs.description:
Creates/modifies wire inputs. All wire ports must begin with an uppercase 
letter and contain only alphanumeric characters.

param.names: An array of input names. May be modified by the function.
param.types:
An array of input types. May be modified by the function. See wire.PORT_TYPE_*. 
Available types: 
- "normal"/"number" 
- "string" 
- "vector" 
- "vector2" 
- "vector4" 
- "angle" 
- "table" 
- "array" 
- "wirelink" 
- "entity"
param.descriptions: An optional array of input descriptions. May be modified by the function.

createOutputs.usage:
-- Creates 2 wire outputs, a string and a number. 
wire.createOutputs( { "MyStringOutput", "MyNumberOutput" }, { "string", "normal" }, { "A string output.", "A number output." } )
createOutputs.description:
Creates/modifies wire outputs. All wire ports must begin with an uppercase 
letter and contain only alphanumeric characters.

param.names: An array of output names. May be modified by the function.
param.types:
An array of output types. May be modified by the function. See wire.PORT_TYPE_*. 
Available types: 
- "normal"/"number" 
- "string" 
- "vector" 
- "vector2" 
- "vector4" 
- "angle" 
- "table" 
- "array" 
- "entity"
param.descriptions: An optional array of output descriptions. May be modified by the function.

delete.ret: True if successful, false, error if not.
delete.description: Deletes the wire leading to the given entity's input.

param.entity: The entity to delete the wire from.
param.input: The name of the input to unwire.

getInputs.ret: Table of entity's inputs, if it has any. Otherwise nil.
getInputs.description: Returns a table of entity's inputs

getOutputs.ret: Table of entity's outputs, if it has any. Otherwise nil.
getOutputs.description: Returns a table of entity's outputs

self.ret: The wirelink for the current instance's entity.
self.description: Returns the wirelink representing the current instance's entity.

ret: True if the name was set, false otherwise.
If the name could not be set, the reason why.
setName.description: Sets the wire name of the entity (i.e. the name used by the wire namer tool).

param.entity: The entity to set the name for.
param.name: The name to assign to the entity.

wire.description: Wire library. Handles wire inputs/outputs, wirelinks, etc.

ports.description:
Ports table. Reads from this table will read from the wire input 
of the same name. Writes will write to the wire output of the same name.

CamData.description: A table describing a view to render from. Used by the "calcview" hook.

angles.desc: The camera's angles (the forward direction determines the direction of the camera).

fov.desc: The field of view of the camera in degrees.

znear.desc: The distance to the near-Z clipping plane (anything closer to the origin than this will not render).

zfar.desc: The distance to the far-Z clipping plane (anything further away from the origin than this will not render).

drawviewer.desc: Whether to draw the player that this hook is executing for. If false, the player's view model will be visible if looking at the player. If true, their world model will be visible instead.

CameraParameters3D.description: Parameters used to start a 3D camera context. All parameters are optional and inherit from the current camera context if omitted (e.g. the player's view).

x.desc: The X position on the 2D surface to draw the 3D world.

y.desc: The Y position on the 2D surface to draw the 3D world.

w.desc: The width to take up on the 2D surface.

h.desc: The height to take up on the 2D surface.

origin.desc: The origin from which the camera will render in 3D space.

angles.desc: The angles determining the direction the camera points in 3D space.

fov.desc: The field of view of the camera.

aspect.desc: The aspect ratio of the camera. If omitted and a width and height are provided, this will be set to width / height.

znear.desc: The distance from the camera to start the near clipping plane.

zfar.desc: The distance from the camera to start the far clipping plane.

ortho.desc: If an orthographic projection is desired, the rectangle that describes it. If omitted, a normal perspective projection camera is used.

CameraRect.description: A box describing a rectangle relative to a point.

left.desc: The left offset of the rectangle.

right.desc: The right offset of the rectangle.

bottom.desc: The bottom offset of the rectangle.

top.desc: The top offset of the rectangle.

Normal.desc: The plane's normal vector (local to the entity).

Origin.desc: The plane's origin (local to the entity).

day.desc: The day of the month (between 1 and 31).

yday.desc: The day of the year (between 1 and 365).

isdst.desc: Whether the date is expressed using daylight-saving time.

name.desc: Name of the environment, if it has one.

air.desc: A table containing percentage levels of various gasses, summing to 100.

Entity.desc: The entity that is responsible for the environment.

radius.desc: The radius that the environment encompasses, if the environment is a planet.

TagColour.desc: The colour of the faction's tag.

LeaderID.desc: The SteamID of the faction leader.

Public.desc: Whether the faction is public or not.

Font.description: Font parameters used when creating fonts.

font.desc: The name of the font to use (e.g. "Arial", "Tahoma", default is "Arial").

size.desc: The size of the font in pixels (between 4 and 255, default is 13).

weight.desc: The weight of the font (how thick it is, minimum of 100, default is 500).

blursize.desc: The strength of blurring to apply (between 0 and 10, default is 0).

antialias.desc: Whether to anti-alias the text, which smooths/blurs the edges (default is true).

underline.desc: Whether to add an underline to the font (default is false).

italic.desc: Whether the font is italic or not (note that fonts that are already italic do not need this, default is false).

strikeout.desc: Whether to strikeout the font (default is false).

symbol.desc: Required when using a symbol font such as Wingdings (default is false).

shadow.desc: Whether to add a shadow to the font (default is false).

additive.desc: Whether the font should render additively or not (default is false).

outline.desc: Whether to add an outline to the font (default is false).

extended.desc: Whether the font can be used to display characters outside the Latin-1 range. Code points past 0xFFFF are not supported (default is true).

HTTPRequest.description: A table describing an HTTP request.

url.desc: The URL to send the request to.

method.desc: The HTTP method to use (case-insensitive). Valid values: GET, HEAD, OPTIONS, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE.

headers.desc: (Optional) Headers to send in the request. When sending a body, specifying a "content-type" header is recommended.

parameters.desc: (Optional) The parameters to encode as a form-encoded body or query parameters. Valid only for GET/HEAD (query parameters) and POST (body) requests. Ignored for POST requests if "body" is provided.

body.desc: (Optional) The request body to send. If set, will override "parameters" on POST requests. Ignored for GET and HEAD requests.

timeout.desc: (Optional) The number of seconds to wait for a response before giving up (default: 60).

onSuccess.desc: The function to call on a successful response, will be invoked with the response body, response headers, and response code.

onFailed.desc: The function to call if the request fails, will be invoked with an error message.

BlurX.desc: How much to blur along the x-axis.

BlurY.desc: How much to blur along the y-axis.

DrawPasses.desc: The number of iterations to draw the halo.

Additive.desc: Whether the halo should be drawn additively or not.

IgnoreZ.desc: Whether the halo should be visible through other objects or not.

HologramClip.description: Clipping plane structure used by holograms.

Normal.desc: The normal direction of the clipping plane.

Origin.desc: The origin of the clipping plane (local or world depending on IsLocal/LocalToEntity).

IsLocal.desc: Flag indicating whether the clipping plane is local to the hologram. If false, the clipping plane may be in world space or local to another entity (see the LocalToEntity field).

LocalToEntity.desc: The entity the clipping plane is local to, if any.

LocalLight.description: A local light definition to be used with render.setLocalModelLights().

type.desc: The type of light to apply (one of render.MATERIAL_LIGHT_*, default = render.MATERIAL_LIGHT_POINT).

color.desc: The colour of the light (x = red, y = green, z = blue). Larger values increase the brightness.

pos.desc: The position of the light in world space.

dir.desc: The direction the light is pointing (applicable to directional and spot lights only).

range.desc: The maximum range of the light source. This does not control attenuation, it merely sets a cutoff point. The default value of 0 is infinite range.

angularFalloff.desc: The angular falloff exponent for spot lights. Higher values result in a sharper transition between the inner and outer cone (default = 5).

innerAngle.desc: The inner cone angle for spot lights, in degrees (default = 45).

outerAngle.desc: The outer cone angle for spot lights, in degrees (default = 45).

fiftyPercentDistance.desc: The distance from the light's position at which the light will be at 50% brightness.

zeroPercentDistance.desc: The distance from the light's position at which the light will fade out entirely.

quadraticFalloff.desc: If the distance values are not supplied, this provides the quadratic term of the falloff (default = 0).

linearFalloff.desc: If the distance values are not supplied, this provides the linear term of the falloff (default = 0).

constantFalloff.desc: If the distance values are not supplied, this provides the constant term of the falloff (default = 1).

MeshVertex.description: Mesh vertex structure used by IMesh:buildFromTriangles().

normal.desc: (Optional) The normal of the vertex, used to calculate lighting.

color.desc: (Optional) The colour to assign to the vertex (for $vertexcolor/$vertexalpha materials).

userdata.desc: (Optional) Tangent data for VertexLitGeneric materials (x, y, z, handedness).

u.desc: The u co-ordinate of the vertex.

v.desc: The v co-ordinate of the vertex.

tangent.desc: (Optional) The tangent of the vertex (not used by VertexLitGeneric materials).

binormal.desc: (Optional) The binormal of the vertex (not used by VertexLitGeneric materials).

LookAheadTimeObjectsVsObject.desc: Maximum amount of seconds to precalculate collisions with objects.

LookAheadTimeObjectsVsWorld.desc: Maximum amount of seconds to precalculate collisions with world.

MaxCollisionsPerObjectPerTimestep.desc: Maximum collision per object per tick.

MaxFrictionMass.desc: Maximum mass of an object to be affected by friction.

MinFrictionMass.desc: Minimum mass of an object to be affected by friction.

DefaultClippingPlanes.desc: A list of clipping planes to apply by default on unclipped entities.

VisualOnly.desc: Whether the clipping planes should be applied visually only.

PropResizerOptions.description: Prop resizer options. All fields are optional.

Mode.desc: The resizer mode to use (one of "ENTITY", "RING" or "TORUS").

UseSize.desc: Whether to enable size-based resizing (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Scale.desc: The scale to configure the tool with, if UseSize is false (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Size.desc: The size to use, if UseSize is true (if Mode is "ENTITY").

YX.desc: The YX shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

ZX.desc: The ZX shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

XY.desc: The XY shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

ZY.desc: The ZY shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

XZ.desc: The XZ shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

YZ.desc: The YZ shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

RingAdvancedMode.desc: Whether to enable advanced ring-resizing options (if Mode is "RING").

RingInnerRadius.desc: The inner radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is false).

RingOuterRadius.desc: The outer radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is false).

RingHeight.desc: The height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is false).

RingInnerLowerHeight.desc: The inner-lower height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

RingInnerUpperHeight.desc: The inner-upper height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

RingOuterLowerHeight.desc: The outer-lower height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

RingOuterUpperHeight.desc: The outer-upper height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

RingInnerLowerRadius.desc: The inner-lower radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

RingOuterLowerRadius.desc: The outer-lower radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

RingInnerUpperRadius.desc: The inner-upper radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

RingOuterUpperRadius.desc: The outer-upper radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

TorusRingRadius.desc: The radius of the torus ring to use (if Mode is "TORUS").

TorusTubeRadius.desc: The radius of the torus tube to use (if Mode is "TORUS").

DefaultText.desc: The default overlay text that would have been rendered.

ServerCPUTime.desc: The CPU time used on the server in the last tick (in microseconds), if a server instance is running.

ServerAverageCPUTime.desc: The average CPU time used on the server (in microseconds), if a server instance is running.

RatioX.desc: Inverted Aspect ratio (height divided by width)

x1.desc: Corner of screen in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

x2.desc: Corner of screen in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

y1.desc: Corner of screen in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

y2.desc: Corner of screen in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

z.desc: Screen plane offset in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

translucent.desc: Whether the screen is set to render without a background.

ScreenParameters.description:
Defines how a screen is rendered in the world, relative to the screen entity. 
All fields are optional, those that are not specified will use their default value.

WidthMultiplier.desc: The multiplier to apply to the screen's width, relative to its height. For example, a value of 2 would mean the screen's width is twice its height.

Offset.desc: The entity-local offset of the screen's centre. This must be within the entity's bounding box.

Angles.desc: The entity-local angles that define how the screen is oriented.

Scale.desc: A scaling factor to apply when drawing the screen, controlling its size. This must not cause the screen to exceed the size of the entity.

Translucent.desc: Whether the screen is translucent or not (if false or nil, a black background is always rendered).

ToScreenData.description: Data indicating screen position of a world position.

x.desc: The x position on the screen.

y.desc: The y position on the screen.

visible.desc: Whether the position is visible or not.

Entity.desc: The entity that the trace hit.

Fraction.desc: How far along the trace ray the hit occurred, from 0-1.

FractionLeftSolid.desc: Given the trace started inside a solid object, this is how far along the ray (0-1) the trace left the solid object.

Hit.desc: Whether the trace hit an obstacle.

HitBox.desc: The hit box ID the trace hit.

HitGroup.desc: The hit group the trace hit.

HitNoDraw.desc: Whether the trace hit a no-draw brush.

HitNonWorld.desc: Whether the trace hit something that is not part of the world.

HitNormal.desc: The normal to the trace's impact position.

HitSky.desc: Whether the trace hit the skybox.

HitTexture.desc: The name of the texture the trace hit.

HitWorld.desc: Whether the trace hit the world.

MatType.desc: The type of material the trace hit.

Normal.desc: The direction vector of the trace as a normalised vector.

PhysicsBone.desc: The physics bone ID that was hit.

StartPos.desc: The starting position of the trace.

StartSolid.desc: Whether the trace started inside a solid object.

AllSolid.desc: Whether the trace is entirely inside a solid object.

SurfaceFlags.desc: The surface flags of the hit surface (see trace.SURF_*).

DispFlags.desc: The displacement flags of the this surface (see trace.DISPSURF_*).

Contents.desc: The contents flags of the hit surface (see trace.CONTENTS_*).

WireOutputConnection.description: Represents a wire output connection to another entity's input.

Name.desc: The name of the input on the connected entity that the output is connected to.

p.desc: The pitch (rotation around the y-axis) of the angle (also available at index 1).

y.desc: The yaw (rotation around the z-axis) of the angle (also available at index 2).

r.desc: The roll (rotation around the x-axis) of the angle (also available at index 3).

mul_Angle_number.ret: A new angle whose values are equal to those in the original angle multiplied by the given number.
mul_Angle_number.description: Multiplies an angle by a scalar value.

div_Angle_number.ret: A new angle whose values are equal to those in the original angle divided by the given number.
div_Angle_number.description: Divides an angle by a scalar value.

add_Angle_Angle.ret: A new angle whose values are the sum of the values in the given vectors.

sub_Angle_Angle.ret: A new angle whose values are the values in the first angle minus those in the second.
sub_Angle_Angle.description: Subtracts one angle's values from another.

unm_Angle_nil.ret: A new angle whose values are the negation of the given angle's.
unm_Angle_nil.description: Returns the negation of the given angle.

eq_Angle_Angle.ret: True if the 2 angles have the same component values, false otherwise.
eq_Angle_Angle.description: Determines if the given angles are equal by checking each component.

Add.description: Adds the given argument angle's component values to the given angle, modifying in-place.

Div.description: Divides the given angle's component values by the given number, modifying in-place.

param.divisor: The number value to divide the angle's components by.

Forward.description: Returns the forward direction for the given angles.

IsZero.description: Returns true if the angle has all components equal to 0.

Mul.description: Multiplies the given angle's component values by the given number, modifying in-place.

param.multiplier: The number value to multiply the angle's components by.

Normalize.description: Normalises the given angle's components into the (-180, 180] range, modifying in-place.

Right.description: Returns the right direction for the given angles.

RotateAroundAxis.description: Rotates the given angles around the given axis by the given amount in degrees, modifying in-place.

param.axis: The axis vector to rotate the angles around.
param.amount: The amount in degrees to rotate by.

Set.description: Sets the angle's component values to equal the given angles, modifying in-place.

param.angle: The angle to take the component values from.

SetUnpacked.description: Sets the angle's component values, modifying in-place.

SnapTo.description: Rounds a component of the given angles to the given amount of degrees.

param.targetDegrees: The amount of degrees to round to (e.g. 90 would round to the nearest multiple of 90 degrees).

Sub.description: Subtracts the given argument angles from the given angles, modifying in-place.

ToTable.description: Returns the angles as an array of 3 elements.

ret: The pitch value.
The yaw value.
The roll value.
Unpack.description: Returns the component values of the angle.

Up.description: Returns the up direction for the given angles.

Zero.description: Sets all component values to 0, modifying in-place..

ChairGhost.description: Chair ghost, representing a TSCM chair tool seating position.

cameraDistance.description: Returns the current camera distance for the vehicle associated with this seating position, or 0 if no player is sat down.

driver.description: Returns the player that is currently sat in this seating position, or nil if no player is sat down.

getExitPos.description: Returns the currently configured exit position for the chair in world space.

passenger.description:
Returns the passenger of this seating position. 
Note that the only valid passenger index is 0, for the player sat in the seat. 
This method is provided to match the Vehicle interface.

param.index: The 0-based passenger index to retrieve.

thirdPersonMode.description: Returns whether the vehicle associated with this seating position is in third person mode or not.

drawModel.description:
Draws the model at the clientside model's position and angles. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

getModelScale.description: Returns the current model scale of the clientside model (as set by setModelScale()).

getRenderMatrix.description: Returns the current rendering matrix for the given clientside model, if it has one.

getScaleVector.description: Returns the current scaling vector applied to the clientside model.

remove.description:
Removes the clientside model. Call this if you don't need it anymore. 
Attempting to use it again will fail.

setAng.description: Sets the clientside model's angles for rendering.

param.ang: Angles in the current 3D world to render with.

param.anim: Animation enum/sequence number, or nil to stop animating.
param.cycle: (Optional) How far through the animation to start (between 0 and 1, default = 0).

setLOD.description: Sets the level of detail for the clientside model.

param.level: A number between 0 and 8 for fixed quality (0 being highest quality), or -1 for automatic based on distance.

setMaterial.description: Sets the material of the clientside model.

setModel.description: Sets the clientside model's model path.

setModelScale.description: Sets the model scale of the given clientside model.

param.deltaTime: The time to take when scaling.

setPos.description: Sets the clientside model's position for rendering.

param.vec: Position in the current 3D world to render at.

setPoseParameter.description: Sets the pose parameter with the given key to the given value.

param.key: The pose parameter ID or name to set.
param.value: The value to set on the given pose parameter.

setRenderMatrix.description: Sets the rendering matrix for the given clientside model.

param.renderMatrix: The matrix to apply to this model when rendering, or nil to reset it.

setRenderMode.description: Sets the render mode of the clientside model.

setScaleVector.description: Sets the scale vector of the given clientside model.

setSkin.description: Sets the skin to use on the clientside model.

setSubMaterial.description: Sets the sub-material at the given index on the clientside model.

param.index: Sub-material index.

Color.description:
Colours in RGB space. 
This is a direct binding to the GLua colour type.

add_Color_Color.ret: A new colour whose values are the sum of those in the given colours.
add_Color_Color.description: Adds the given colour values field-by-field (including alpha).

sub_Color_Color.ret: A new colour whose values are the second colour's values subtracted from the first.
sub_Color_Color.description: Subtracts the second colour's values from the first field-by-field (including alpha).

mul_Color_number.ret: A new colour whose values are multiplied by the given number.
mul_Color_number.description: Multiplies the given colour's RGBA values by the given number.

div_Color_number.ret: A new colour whose values are divided by the given number.
div_Color_number.description: Divides the given colour's RGBA values by the given number.

SetUnpacked.description: Sets the RGBA components of the colour.

ret: The hue of the colour in degrees between 0 and 360.
The saturation of the colour between 0 and 1.
The lightness of the colour between 0 and 1.
ToHSL.description: Returns the colour in HSL space.

ret: The hue of the colour in degrees between 0 and 360.
The saturation of the colour between 0 and 1.
The value of the colour between 0 and 1.
ToHSV.description: Returns the colour in HSV space.

ret: The hue of the colour between 0 and 1.
The saturation of the colour between 0 and 1.
The value of the colour between 0 and 1.
ToNormalizedHSV.description: Returns the colour in HSV space, normalising the hue to between 0 and 1.

ToTable.description: Returns the colour as an (r, g, b, a) array.

ToVector.description: Returns the RGB values as a Vector, with each component normalised to be between 0 and 1.

ret: The red value.
The green value.
The blue value.
The alpha value.
Unpack.description: Returns the components of the colour.

setAngles.description: Sets the angles of the effect data object.

setEntity.description: Sets the entity of the effect data object.

setMagnitude.description: Sets the magnitude of the effect data object.

setOrigin.description: Sets the origin of the effect data object.

setRadius.description: Sets the radius of the effect data object.

setScale.description: Sets the scale of the effect data object.

setStart.description: Sets the start of the effect data object.

addCallback.usage:
-- Adding a PhysicsCollide callback 
Entity:addCallback( "PhysicsCollide", "MyUniqueName", function( Ent, CollisionData ) 
	print( Ent, "hit", CollisionData.HitEntity, "with speed", CollisionData.Speed ) 
end )
addCallback.description: Adds a callback to an entity for a specific event. Valid events are: - PhysicsCollide

param.name: The name of the event to add a callback for.
param.func: The function to call when the event is fired.

ang.description: Returns the angle of the entity

attachments.description: Returns a table of all attachments the entity's model has, or nil if it has none.

boundingRadius.description:
Returns the distance to the furthest corner on the entity's bounding box. 
Note that this will return 0 on clientside models as it is derived from collision data.

callOnRemove.description: Registers a callback to run on removal of this entity.

param.identifier: A unique string identifier for this callback. Used if you want to remove it later.
param.callback: The function to run when the entity is removed. Receives the entity, then the arguments passed to this.
Arguments to pass to the callback.

class.description: Returns the class of the entity

coreHealth.description: Returns the health of the ship core the entity is assigned to.

coreMaxHealth.description: Returns the maximum health of the ship core the entity is assigned to.

createWire.ret: True if successful, false, error if not.
createWire.description: Wires the input of self to the output of Ent.

param.input: The name of the input on self to wire.
param.output: The name of the output on Ent to wire to.

deleteWire.ret: True if successful, false, error if not.
deleteWire.description: Deletes the wire leading to self's input.

param.input: The name of the input to unwire.

followBone.description: Parents the entity to the given parent's bone.

param.parent: The parent entity to attach this entity to.
param.boneID: The ID of the bone on the parent to attach to.

forward.description: Gets the entity's forward direction vector.

getAttachment.ret: A table with fields "Pos" and "Ang", or nil if no such attachment exists.
getAttachment.description: Returns a table containing the position and angles of the given attachment, if it exists.

param.attachment: The attachment name to get the position/angle for.

getBodygroup.description: Gets the bodygroup the current ID is using.

getBoneCount.ret: The number of bones on the entity, or -1 if the number is not known.
getBoneCount.description: Gets the number of bones the entity's model has defined.

--Iterate over all lines. 
for Line in File:lines() do

setRenderCallback.description:
Sets or removes the render callback for the hologram. 
The provided callback function will be invoked with the hologram, and a bitflag indicating the type of rendering being performed (see render.STUDIO_*). 
Note that the hologram's model will not render unless the callback invokes Hologram:drawModel(). 
All clipping, lighting and material overrides will be applied to whatever is rendered in the callback. 
This callback may be invoked multiple times per frame, depending on the render group of the hologram, and whether water reflections are being drawn.
setRenderCallback.usage:
Holo:setRenderCallback( function( Holo, Flags ) 
	if bit.band( Flags, render.STUDIO_TRANSPARENCY ) ~= 0 then 
		-- Render translucent pass (e.g. sprites). 
		render.setMaterial( SpriteMaterial ) 
		render.drawSprite( Holo:pos(), 64, 64, White ) 
		-- If the hologram has translucent materials, also render its model here. The engine will handle rendering 
		-- only the translucent parts. 
	else 
		-- Render opaque pass (e.g. the hologram's opaque model). 
		Holo:drawModel() 
	end 
end )

param.callback: The callback to set as the render callback, or nil to reset rendering back to the default behaviour.

Panel.description:
Panel 
Note that most methods are from the panel object itself, so you'll need to look up GLua's documentation on panels to find them. 
There are a few methods that are not exposed, or are modified slightly. See the panel-specific types for modified methods.

Quaternion.description:
Quaternion 
Fields are stored as r, i, j, k components respectively and can be indexed with 1-4 or the field's name.

r.desc: The real part of the quaternion.

i.desc: The first imaginary part of the quaternion.

j.desc: The second imaginary part of the quaternion.

k.desc: The third imaginary part of the quaternion.

w.desc: The real part of the quaternion (alias of 'r').

x.desc: The first imaginary part of the quaternion (alias of 'i').

y.desc: The second imaginary part of the quaternion (alias of 'j').

z.desc: The third imaginary part of the quaternion (alias of 'k').

abs.description: Returns the absolute value of the quaternion.

conj.ret: The conjugate of this quaternion as a new quaternion.
conj.description: Returns the conjugate of the quaternion.

exp.ret: e raised to the power of the given quaternion.
exp.description: Returns Euler's constant e raised to the power of the quaternion.

forward.description: Returns a vector pointing forward, relative to the rotation represented by the quaternion.

inv.ret: The inverse of this quaternion as a new quaternion.
inv.description: Returns the inverse of the quaternion.

mod.ret: A new quaternion with a normalised angle.
mod.description: Changes the quaternion so that the represented rotation is by an angle between 0 and 180 degrees.

right.description: Returns a vector pointing right, relative to the rotation represented by the quaternion.

setUnpacked.description: Sets the values of the quaternion.

param.i: The i component value to set.
param.j: The j component value to set.
param.k: The k component value to set.

ret: The real part.
The i component.
The j component.
The k component.
unpack.description: Returns the 4 components of the quaternion.

up.description: Returns a vector pointing up, relative to the rotation represented by the quaternion.

setMaterialLinearWrite.usage:
-- Create a render target and draw a basic white square. 
local RT = rendertarget.createWithSize( 512, 512 ) 
-- Set the render target's material to use linear writes, disabling gamma correction. 
RT:setMaterialLinearWrite( true ) 
RT:drawToTexture( function() 
	-- Note: inside the render target, the linear write flag on the RT has **no effect**. 
	-- Use render.getLinearWriteMaterial() to draw shapes without gamma correction here. 
	-- Clearing is not affected by gamma correction, as it directly writes the pixel values. 
	render.clear( 255, 255, 255, 255 ) 
end ) 
 
hook( "render", "test", function() 
	render.clear( 0, 0, 0, 255 ) 
	-- As the material disables gamma correction, this will draw an exactly 16, 16, 16 colour square. 
	-- If linear writes were disabled, this would instead draw an 8, 8, 8 colour square. 
	render.setColor( 16, 16, 16, 255 ) 
	render.setMaterial( RT ) 
	render.drawTexturedRect( 0, 0, 128, 128 ) 
end )
setMaterialLinearWrite.description:
Sets whether the render target's material should be drawn using linear pixel writes (i.e. without gamma correction). 
This applies to drawing the render target as a material and applying a colour modulation in a 2D surface context, such as on a screen or within another render target. This does **not** affect render operations applied within the render target itself. 
The render target must be using the rendertarget.SHADER_UNLIT_GENERIC shader to apply this setting.

param.linearWrite: Whether the render target's material should use linear writes.

setShader.description:
Sets the shader to use to render this render target in future rendering operations. 
Note that 2D rendering can only use rendertarget.SHADER_UNLIT_GENERIC. Attempting to render in 2D with a different shader will fail. 
Also, note that material flags are separate between shaders, so they should be configured after calling this method.

The exact format of the scan output depends on the entity being scanned. This operation is separate to the sensor's current target, which is continuously updated. Deep scans return more information, such as type information on weapons. 
Each successful deep scan performed costs energy (see SensorArray:getDeepScanEnergyCost()) and has a maximum range (see SensorArray:getMaxDeepScanRange()).

Returns the forward direction of the matrix. 
This is the first column of the matrix excluding the 4th component.

Returns the inverse matrix of the given matrix. 
This operation will fail for matrices with determinant close to or equal to 0.

getInverseTR.ret: The matrix representing the inverse of the given matrix, assumming the matrix was not scaled.
getInverseTR.description:
Returns the inverse matrix of the given matrix. 
This function will not fail, but only works correctly on matrices that contain only translation and/or rotation.

getRight.ret: A vector representing the right direction.
getRight.description:
Returns the right direction of the matrix. 
This is the second column of the matrix excluding the 4th component.

getTransposed.description: Returns a copy of the matrix with its rows tranposed (every row becomes a column).

getUp.ret: A vector representing the up direction.
getUp.description:
Returns the up direction of the matrix. 
This is the third column of the matrix excluding the 4th component.

identity.description:
Resets the matrix's values to the identity matrix. 
Modifies the given matrix directly.

invert.description:
Inverts the given matrix, modifying in-place. 
This operation will fail for matrices with determinant close to or equal to 0. If it does fail, the matrix is not modified.

invertTR.description:
Inverts the given matrix, modifying in-place. 
This function will not fail, but only works correctly on matrices that contain only translation and/or rotation.

isIdentity.ret: True if the matrix is the identity matrix, false otherwise.
isIdentity.description: Returns whether the given matrix is the identity matrix.

isRotationMatrix.description:
Returns whether the given matrix is a "rotation matrix". 
This checks whether the forward, right and up directions are orthogonal and normalised to 1.

mul.description: Post-multiplies this matrix by the given matrix (i.e. self * matrix), setting this matrix's fields to the result.

rotate.description: Rotates the matrix by the given angle. This is equivalent to post-multiplying by a rotation matrix, but without creating a new matrix object.

scale.description: Scales the matrix by the given vector scale. This is equivalent to post-multiplying by a scaled matrix, but without creating a new matrix object.

scaleTranslation.description: Scales the absolute translation of the matrix by the given scale.

set.description: Copies the values from the given matrix object to self.

param.matrix: The matrix to copy values from.

setAngles.description: Sets the angles of the matrix.

setField.description: Sets the value at the given row and column.

param.row: The row to change the value of.
param.column: The column to change the value of.

setForward.description:
Sets the forward direction of the matrix. 
This changes the first 3 values in the first column of the matrix.

setRight.description:
Sets the right direction of the matrix. 
This changes the first 3 values in the second column of the matrix.

setScale.description: Sets the scale of the matrix (the 3 diagonal values).

setTranslation.description: Sets the translation (the first 3 rows of the 4th column).

setUnpacked.description: Sets the values of all fields in the matrix.

setUp.description:
Sets the up direction of the matrix. 
This changes the first 3 values in the third column of the matrix.

sub.description: Subtracts the given matrix component-wise from this matrix.

param.matrix: The matrix to subtract from this matrix.

toTable.ret: Table containing 4 tables representing the rows of the matrix.
toTable.description: Returns a 4x4 table representing the values of the matrix.

translate.description: Translates the matrix by the given amount. This is equivalent to post-multiplying by a translation matrix, but without creating a new matrix object.

transpose.description: Tranposes the matrix (every row becomes a column), modifying in-place.

autoupdate.description:
Marks the entity to automatically update its files. 
If --@updater is set, the newest version in the shared area will be used both after spawning normally and from the duplicator if it is available (with read access). 
Otherwise, the newest version stored in the owner's local files will be used.

param.path: An optional path to the file in the shared area that this file should be updated with on upload. This can be used to create a local alias for a shared file at a different path.

class.description:
Sets what type of entity is allowed to run this script. 
Attempting to spawn the script on an entity that is not in the list will result in an error message. 
Valid classes are: processor, screen, hud, emitter. 
In addition, if the value is set to "none", the script will be blocked from spawning with any type. This can be useful for library files.

param.classes: A list of comma separated classes to allow to run this script.

dependency.description:
Add a dependency on a shared file or folder. 
Files will be pulled in from the shared area belonging to the specified Steam ID, or your own if no Steam ID is given. 
Note that if you do not have permission to read a file, you'll get an error. 
When requesting folders, only 'async' and 'sv' are applicable to configure how the files are networked, all files must be manually executed. Also note that folders are not included recursively.
dependency.usage:
--@dependency lib/myServerLibrary.txt sv 
--@dependency STEAM_0:0:1234 lib/theirUsefulLib.txt sh 
--@dependency coolstuff/requiredlater.txt 
--@dependency morestuff/in/a/folder sv 
--@dependency secret/example.txt async dev 
 
-- lib/myServerLibrary.txt is loaded on the server 
-- lib/theirUsefulLib.txt is loaded shared from STEAM_0:0:1234's shared folder. 
-- coolstuff/requiredlater.txt is included but not loaded yet. 
-- All files directly inside 'morestuff/in/a/folder' are included on the server only, but not loaded. 
-- secret/example.txt is included but not downloaded by clients until required, and will be uploaded from local 
-- files if available. 
if SERVER then 
	require( "coolstuff/requiredlater.txt" ) 
elseif ents.owner() == ents.player() then 
	require( "secret/example.txt" ) 
end

param.steamID: Steam ID whose shared folder the file/folder should be pulled from.
param.path: The path to the file/folder you want to include.
param.modifier: Optional modifier to determine how a file is loaded or a folder's files are networked. One of 'sv', 'sh', 'cl' or 'async' for server auto-require, shared auto-require, client auto-require and asynchronous include respectively.
param.mode: Optional mode to resolve the dependency with. Specify 'dev' to first attempt to load a local file with the given name. By default, local files are ignored.

highres.description:
Sets whether the screen should be high resolution. 
High resolution screens are 1024 x 1024, while normal resolution screens are 512 x 512.

include.description:
Mark a file to be included in the upload. 
This is required to use the file in require() and dofile()

includeasync.description: Mark a file to be included in the upload but not downloaded to clients until executed by require() or dofile().
includeasync.usage:
--@includeasync example.txt 
 
if ents.player() == ents.owner() then 
	print( time.curTime(), "Loading example file asynchronously..." ) 
 
	-- This will yield until the file has been downloaded. 
	require( "example.txt" ) 
 
	print( time.curTime(), "Example file loaded." ) 
end 
-- CODE

includedir.description: Mark a folder to be included in the upload. This is not recursive.

includedirasync.description: Mark a folder to be included in the upload but not downloaded to clients until each file is executed by require() or dofile(). This is not recursive.
includedirasync.usage:
--@includedirasync lib 
 
if ents.player() == ents.owner() then 
	require( "lib/someLibrary.txt" ) 
	require( "lib/someOtherLibrary.txt" ) 
end 
-- CODE

includesv.description: Mark a file to be included in the upload, but not sent to clients for download. Use this for files that will only be used on the server.
includesv.usage:
--@includesv lib/someLibrary.txt 
if SERVER then 
	require( "lib/someLibrary.txt" ) 
end 
-- CODE

mainfile.description:
Marks the script as only runnable as the main file. 
Any instance attempting to load with this script as an include rather than the main script will error before running.
mainfile.usage:
--@mainfile 
-- This can only be executing if the file is the mainfile. 
assert( _MAINFILE == starfall.getMainFileName() )

model.description: Sets the model of a processor entity only.

param.path: Path of the model to use.

name.description:
Set the name of the script. 
This will become the name of the tab and will show on the overlay of the processor

param.name: Name of the script.

precache.description:
Precaches a sound or model file. 
This ensures that playing the sound or creating a prop with the model does not use a large amount of CPU time.

param.paths: A comma-separated list of file paths (.wav, .ogg, .mp3, .mdl) to precache.

require.description: Mark a file to be included in the upload and then required on both the server and the client.
require.usage:
--@require lib/someLibrary.txt 
-- Your file is already required and ready to use here.

requirecl.description: Mark a file to be included in the upload and then required on the client only.
requirecl.usage:
--@requirecl lib/someLibrary.txt 
if CLIENT then 
-- Your file is already required and ready to use here. 
end

requiresv.description: Mark a file to be included in the upload and then required on the server only. This file will not be sent to clients.
requiresv.usage:
--@requiresv lib/someLibrary.txt 
if SERVER then 
-- Your file is already required and ready to use here. 
end

resolution.description:
Sets the screen resolution in pixels. 
Valid values are 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024 or 2048 for each dimension. The default resolution is 512 x 512. 
Higher resolutions can be more costly to render, especially for lower-end systems.

param.width: The width of the screen in pixels.
param.height: The height of the screen in pixels.

shared.description:
For screens, HUDs and emitters, make the script run on the server alongside the client. 
You can use "if SERVER then" and "if CLIENT then" to determine if the script is currently being run on the server or the client, respectively.
shared.usage:
--@shared 
 
if SERVER then 
	-- Do important calculations 
	-- Send net message 
else 
	-- Display result of important calculations 
end

updater.description:
Sets the Steam ID of the shared area folder to check for updates against. 
This allows users of your script to right click the file in the editor and choose "Update". 
They will then download the latest version of this file and all included files from your shared folder.

param.steamID: The SteamID of the player responsible for updating this script.

widescreen.description:
Sets whether the screen should be widescreen. 
Widescreen screens are 1024 x 512, while normal resolution screens are 512 x 512.
